2604.13627.title: (How) Learning Rates Regulate Catastrophic Overtraining
2604.13627.paper_summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is a common first stage of LLM post-training, teaching the model to follow instructions and shaping its behavior as a helpful assistant. At the same time, SFT may harm the fundamental capabilities of an LLM, particularly after long pretraining: a phenomenon known as catastrophic overtraining (Springer et al., 2025). To understand overtraining, we first investigate catastrophic forgetting in finetuning through the lens of implicit regularization of the learning rate. For models trained to the same SFT loss, we identify how the learning rate mediates optimization: finetuning with large and small steps converges to qualitatively different models. Next, we link forgetting to overtraining: learning rate decay increases the sharpness of the pretrained model, which in turn exacerbates catastrophic forgetting during SFT, leading to overtraining. Our findings paint a picture of the overtraining mechanism in LLMs and broadly contribute to the understanding of the interplay between optimization dynamics during pretraining and finetuning.
2604.13627.paper_summary_zh: 監督式微調（SFT）是大型語言模型（LLM）後訓練的常見第一階段，教導模型遵循指令並塑造其作為有用助手的行為。<lb/>同時，SFT 可能會損害 LLM 的基本能力，特別是在長時間的預訓練之後：這種現象被稱為災難性過度訓練（Springer et al., 2025）。<lb/>為了理解過度訓練，我們首先通過學習率的隱性正則化來研究微調中的災難性遺忘。<lb/>對於訓練到相同 SFT 損失的模型，我們確定學習率如何調節優化：使用大步驟和小步驟的微調收斂到質量上不同的模型。<lb/>接下來，我們將遺忘與過度訓練聯繫起來：學習率衰減增加了預訓練模型的尖銳度，進而加劇了 SFT 過程中的災難性遺忘，導致過度訓練。<lb/>我們的研究結果描繪了 LLM 中過度訓練的機制，並廣泛促進了對預訓練和微調過程中優化動態相互作用的理解。
2604.13627.author: Mark Rofin et.al.
2604.13627.authors: Mark Rofin, Aditya Varre, Nicolas Flammarion
